original:
"Sure. And they've been churning out garbage for 200 cycles. But what we see here is that this was made by a very old, very large forge. Probably top of the line for the time. And your sample has been exposed to space for a long, long time. And no FRM."

"Haven't seen this before." She rubs her fingers together, thinking. "Either someone was running a starsystem polity-level illicit forge a couple thousand cycles ago and you found the results, or... hm, you found something really weird."

She turns to you. "Where'd you find this? Some rock in the Abyss?"

option_line: oyaTanaica_vambrace8:"Some rock. Why can't it have been legally forged with no FRM?"
original: Some rock. Why can't it have been legally forged with no FRM?

"That's... that'd be insane. The whole point of the Domain was to control this stuff! Well," she concedes to herself, "Maybe the Gate Network was point number one."

"But regulating the nanoforges would have been two. Maybe three. Definitely important. Everything is stamped and tracked, that way you know who made what, where it ends up, when to collect forge-taxes, and who to blame when things go wrong."

"It was..." She shakes her hands at the analysis suite in an odd gesture, emphasizing the size of the concept. "It was the basis for interstellar civilization itself!"

She takes a deep breath, looking self-conscious at her own outburst, then busies herself with flicking at some controls; the machine slows its hum to a dull whine and then a sudden unsettling absence of a background noise you weren't even conscious of hearing.

"Some rebel polity in the Domain-era I guess? Maybe they had some frontier-freedom notions before the Domain Armada re-stomped them into compliance. I'd pay good credits myself to learn how in Ludd's seven hells it ended up on some rock in the Abyss!"

She catches herself, then grins. "Uh, not that I wouldn't appreciate that answer for free if you happen to solve it."

option_line: oyaTanaica_vambraceOneslaught1:"Have you heard of the Onslaught mk.I?"
original: Have you heard of the Onslaught mk.I?

The sample emerges, and - more gingerly, now - Senior Engineer Tanaica pulls on some gloves and places it back in your sample container.

"You find some interesting stuff out there if you don't mind me saying so, captain."

She nods approvingly. "Thank you for the subsidy. I'll be sure to enjoy myself once I wrap up this miserable Asher contract."

"Oh sure," she says.

The sample emerges, and - more gingerly, now - Senior Engineer Tanaica pulls on some gloves and places it back in your sample container.

"Wait." She looks at the sample container. "Really?"
"Wow." She hands the sample container back to you.
"Wow. Guess that explains why the newscasts have been so boring today. Luddics, right?" She hands the sample container back to you.

"You find some interesting stuff out there if you don't mind me saying so, captain."

She nods approvingly. "Thank you for the subsidy. I'll be sure to enjoy myself once I wrap up this miserable Asher contract."
